summary: Dogfood found opennori init produced empty .opennori state directories plus an agent-guide that assumed an existing Architecture Baseline, confusing users in a newly initialized project. The generated agent guide now states empty directories are normal, tells agents not to implement before a Nori Contract exists, and only asks them to read Architecture Baseline files when they exist or non-trivial work requires one. Init/bootstrap user-facing messages also explain that no active Nori Contract exists yet.
reviewability: Initialize a temporary project and inspect .opennori/agent-guide.md; verify doctor remains ready after managed asset upgrade; rerun npm run check.
limitations: Existing projects with the old generated guide need opennori upgrade --dry-run and --confirm to refresh the managed guide.
